actionUnits.condition: Pre-check and bind: Confirm the MonoBehaviour on this object matches script guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d (Fire). Scene values for this instance: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, downMultiplier=2, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo=fileID 1849464941111748095, BoxCollider enabled, no Rigidbody. Note: PrefabInstance modifications also indicate debug=1 and maxNeighborDistance=6 overriding prefab defaults; ensure these are applied at runtime. This object has a Collider, so Trigger actions are valid. This establishes that the provided MonoBehaviour component settings are the exact settings used by the attached Fire script on this GameObject.

Ignite the fire so that WaterHit logic can be tested. Do one of: (A) externally call Fire.SetIsOn(true) on MonoBehaviour (script guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d), or (B) toggle the serialized isOn to true at runtime via test harness. Expected immediate effect: pSystem.Play() is called via UpdateFireState(false).

Hit the fire with water once to start extinguish timer, then wait longer than maxHitInterval to verify cancellation branch. At t=0 invoke Fire.WaterHit(normal) via the water system (any normal vector, e.g., Vector3.up). Expect isTurningOff=true, currentHitInterval reset to 0. After 2.1s (> maxHitInterval=2), Update() should cancel turning off (isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime reset to 0) and the fire remains on.

Extinguish path: Apply first water hit within interval budget. At t=0 invoke Fire.WaterHit(normal) again (e.g., Vector3.up). Expect isTurningOff=true; currentHitInterval=0; currentHitTime accumulates in Update(). Keep spacing next hits < maxHitInterval.
Second water hit (<= 1s after previous). Invoke Fire.WaterHit(normal) to reset currentHitInterval, continue accumulating currentHitTime toward totalHitTime=5.
Third water hit (<= 1s after previous). Invoke Fire.WaterHit(normal).
Fourth water hit (<= 1s after previous). Invoke Fire.WaterHit(normal).
Fifth water hit (<= 1s after previous). Invoke Fire.WaterHit(normal).
Sixth water hit (<= 1s after previous). Invoke Fire.WaterHit(normal). By now, cumulative currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s) while currentHitInterval never exceeded 2s.

Allow Update() to process the extinguish threshold. Expected: Fire turns off (isOn=false), shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime reset, UpdateFireState(true) stops pSystem and notifies neighbors; FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1) is called.

Re-ignition via neighbor influence. Arrange at least 2 neighbors in neighbors list to call NotifyNeighborState(true) on this instance (exceed onNeighborsThreshold=2). Ensure at least one neighbor is below this fire by > minNeighborDownDistance=3 to set hasDownNeighbor=true (so downMultiplier=2 speeds time). With shouldTurnOn=true, let time elapse until timeToOnDelta >= timeToOn. Because Awake() randomizes timeToOn in [40,60], with downMultiplier=2 the effective wait can be ~20-30s. Wait 25s. Expected: fire turns on (isOn=true), timers reset, UpdateFireState(true) plays pSystem and notifies neighbors; FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1) is called.
